data.title: Oil-Sensitive Stocks Jump, Defense Dives in Market Pivot as Cease-Fire Optimism Picks Up — Market Talk
data.description: Stocks that would benefit from lower oil prices and an opening of the Strait of Hormuz turn to sharp gains in midmorning European trade as faith grows that a fragile U.S.-Iran cease-fire would hold. Traders may be looking through both sides' brinkmanship and taking an optimistic view on weekend negotiations to end the conflict, XTB's Kathleen Brooks writes. Airline Wizz Air is up 11.5% while German industrial groups Siemens and Heidelberg Materials gain 3% and 4.25%, respectively, after pivoting at around 0915 GMT. Conversely, defense stocks fall sharply. Rheinmetall is 5.9% lower while Hensoldt slides 5.5% following a similar about-turn. French defense group Thales also drops, with the stock down 3.1% for the session. (josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com)

data.title: GSK Could Report Weak General Medicines Results — Market Talk
data.description: GSK's first-quarter results could be modestly negative, with U.S. prescriptions trends pointing to a weak performance at the U.K. drugmaker's general medicines segment, UBS analysts say in a research note. HIV treatment Cabenuva, lupus drug Benlysta and respiratory medicine Nucala are likely to deliver continued positive top-line momentum, UBS says. On the other hand, UBS estimates general medicines sales will be 4% below the consensus forecast. "We question whether an increase in patient out-of-pocket burden--copay or coinsurance--early in the year for respiratory products may have resulted in a higher level of prescription abandonment," the analysts say. Updates on the early uptake of recently launched drugs Blenrep and Exdensur as well as the HIV strategy will be in focus, UBS says. Shares rise 1.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: UK gilts slide sharply again as oil prices climb back up
data.description:
By Andy Bruce

British government bond prices fell for a second day on Friday as oil prices climbed again on worries about damage to energy infrastructure in Saudi Arabia caused by the Iran war, with long-dated gilts suffering the biggest drops.

Gilts underperformed similar U.S., German and French debt, reflecting Britain's vulnerability to fallout from rising energy prices because of its reliance on natural gas and stretched public finances, which could make state support hard to afford.

Fiscal worries have increasingly hurt long-dated gilts.

The 20-year gilt yield (GB20YT=RR), which moves inversely to the price, was up 8 basis points on the day at 1018 GMT - on track for a small weekly increase having slumped on Wednesday on news of a ceasefire between Iran and the United States.

While optimism that the ceasefire will hold provided a modest boost to share prices on Friday, global bond markets were dented by rising oil prices on the back of a report that attacks on Saudi energy facilities had cut the kingdom's oil production.

Furthermore, tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains largely frozen.

Emma Moriarty, portfolio manager at CG Asset Management, said spikes in gilt yields had become more frequent in recent years as high levels of public debt and anaemic economic growth left the UK economy extremely vulnerable to shocks.

She said inflation-linked bonds were performing better than conventional gilts - unlike in the "mini-budget" crisis of 2022 which was driven by worries about the fiscal cost of former prime minister Liz Truss' tax cut plans.

"As inflation fears have been the principal cause of this recent selloff, investors have been prepared to pay more for inflation-protected bonds, particularly at the short end of the curve where our funds are positioned," Moriarty said.

Short- and medium-dated gilt yields were up around 5-7 basis points on the day.

data.title: Taking Stock: Markets bounce back; Nifty reclaims 24,000, Sensex jumps 900 pts; IT worst hit
data.description:
After a weak closing in the previous session, the Indian equity indices rebounded strongly on April 10, ending with solid gains, supported by positive global cues amid easing geopolitical concerns.

The market opened on a gap-up note and witnessed broad-based buying across sectors, except IT. This momentum helped the Nifty 50 index to hits 24,074.05 mark intraday, before it settled near the day’s high.

At close, the Sensex was up 918.60 points or 1.20 percent at 77,550.25, and the Nifty was up 275.50 points or 1.16 percent at 24,050.60.

Biggest Nifty gainers were Asian Paints, Eicher Motors, ICICI Bank, Bajaj Auto, Shriram Finance, while losers included Coal India, Sun Pharma, Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra.

Among sectors, Auto, Capital Goods, Consumer Durables, Realty, Power, FMCG, PSU Bank, Metal, PSU Bank, Private Bank added 1-2%, while IT index shed 1.7%.

Broader indices outperformed the benchmark indices, with the Nifty Midcap and Smallcap indices rising 1.5 percent each.

For the week, the BSE Sensex and Nifty gained nearly 6 percent each, marking their best weekly performance since February 2021, while also snapping a six-week losing streak.

More than 100 stocks touched their 52-week high on the BSE, including Adani Energy, Ather Energy, ABB India, Hitachi Energy, Cummins India, Titan Company, MCX India, Anand Rathi, Natco Pharma, among others. Click to View More

In stock-specific action, Wipro shares rose 1% after the company announced that its board will consider a share buyback. Dev Information Technology added 7% after securing a ₹26 crore contract, while Shares of Power Mech Projects gained 9% following an order win worth ₹296 crore.

On the downside, Tata Consultancy Services declined 2.5% after reporting its Q4 earnings and Sun Pharmaceutical Industries fell 3.6% amid reports of a potential acquisition of Organon.

Meanwhile, HDFC Life Insurance Company shares rose 2% as its board is set to consider a fundraising proposal on April 16. Shares of Godrej Properties jumped 1.5% after the company reported its highest-ever bookings and collections for FY2026.

In other developments, Eicher Motors gained 3.2% following the launch of its first electric motorcycle, while IRB Infrastructure Developers rose 3% as toll revenue increased 20.7% in March 2026.

Shares of Saatvik Green Energy surged 10% after securing an order worth ₹108.75 crore. RailTel Corporation of India advanced 2% on winning a ₹23.18 crore contract, and Shriram Finance gained 3% after CRISIL upgraded its long-term rating to AAA with a stable outlook.

data.title: Raw sugar steadies near one-month low amid ample supplies
data.description:
Raw sugar futures on ICE steadied near a one-month low on Friday, heading for 7% losses on the week as investors focused on ample supplies and put aside rising energy prices and the Iran war, for now.

Higher energy prices are bullish for sugar because they can prompt cane mills to lower sugar output in favour of ethanol, a cane-based biofuel.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar ​​edged up 0.4% at 13.99 cents per lb​​ at 1011 GMT, not far off Thursday's one month low of 13.90 cents.

* "The market remains more sensitive to its own supply and demand balance than to external drivers," said broker StoneX.

* "Sugar continues to show weakness, as during the recent (futures market) rally the export premium (for physical sugar) in Santos did not follow. On the contrary, (it) declined," it added.

* China, the world's second-largest sugar importer, has raised its forecast for 2025/26 sugar output by 800,000 metric tons from the previous month to 12.5 million.

* Looking ahead, supplies might tighten as the U.S. Climate Prediction Center has forecast the El Niño weather phenomenon has a 61% chance of developing between May and June 2026 and is expected to persist through at least the end of 2026.

* White sugar ​​rose 0.9% to $417.50 a ton, having hit its lowest since mid-March on Thursday.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee dipped 0.3% to $2.9270 per lb​​, having hit a one-month low this week.

* February coffee exports from Uganda, the world's sixth- largest producer, rose 17.3% compared with the same period last year, helped by higher production, the ministry of agriculture said.

* Coffee is under pressure more broadly from expectations for an ample crop this year from top grower Brazil.

* Robusta coffee fell 1.2% to $3,270 a ton, having hit its lowest since last July at $3,265.

COCOA

* London cocoa rose 1.3% to 2,398 pounds a metric ton.

* Dealers said there is little correlation between El Niño and rainfall in top cocoa-growing region West Africa, though the weather phenomenon could lead to high temperatures and dips in production in Ecuador and Indonesia.

* Overall though, cocoa remains rangebound amid weak demand and a modest supply recovery, both of which are seen as priced in for now.

* New York cocoa ​​rose 1.8% to $3,218 a ton.

data.title: India to delay maintenance shut down of 10,000 MW coal-fired power capacity to July
data.description:
India will shut about 10,000 megawatts of coal-fired power generation capacity in July for maintenance, a delay of three months as the country seeks to meet its summer power demand, Piyush Singh, additional secretary in the federal power ministry, said on Friday.

He said about 8,000 megawatts of gas-fired generation capacity is hit due to higher fuel costs under the impact of the Iran war.
